name: cockroach
description: A Pulumi package for managing CockroachDB Cloud resources.
attribution: This Pulumi package is based on the [`cockroach` Terraform Provider](https://github.com/cockroachdb/terraform-provider-cockroach).

nodejs.packageDescription: A Pulumi package for managing CockroachDB Cloud resources.
nodejs.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/cockroachdb/terraform-provider-cockroach)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-cockroach` repo](https://github.com/nellisauction/pulumi-cockroach/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-cockroach` repo](https://github.com/cockroachdb/terraform-provider-cockroach/issues).

python.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/cockroachdb/terraform-provider-cockroach)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-cockroach` repo](https://github.com/nellisauction/pulumi-cockroach/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-cockroach` repo](https://github.com/cockroachdb/terraform-provider-cockroach/issues).

apijwt.description:
The JWT from a JWT Issuer configured for the CockroachDB Cloud Organization.
In this case, the vanity name of the organization is required and can be provided using the `COCKROACH_VANITY_NAME` environment variable. If the JWT is mapped to multiple identities, the identity to impersonate should be provided using the `COCKROACH_USERNAME` environment variable, and should contain either a user email address or a service account ID.

apikey.description: The API key to access CockroachDB Cloud. If this field is provided, it is used and `apijwt` is ignored.

enabled.description: Indicates whether backups are enabled. If set to false, no backups will be created.

frequencyMinutes.description: The frequency of backups in minutes.  Valid values are [5, 10, 15, 30, 60, 240, 1440]

retentionDays.description: The number of days to retain backups for.  Valid values are [2, 7, 30, 90, 365]. Can only be set once, further changes require opening a support ticket. See Updating backup retention for more information.

arn.description: The AWS IAM Role ARN that CockroachDB Cloud will assume in your account.

subscriptionId.description: The Azure subscription ID in the customer-owned tenant.

tenantId.description: The customer-owned Azure tenant ID that contains the subscription.

serviceAccountEmail.description: The customer-owned GCP service account email CockroachDB Cloud will impersonate.

cidrRange.description: The IPv4 range in CIDR format that will be used by the cluster. This is supported only on GCP, and must have a subnet mask no larger than /19. Defaults to "172.28.0.0/14". This cannot be changed after cluster creation.

diskIops.description: Number of disk I/O operations per second that are permitted on each node in the cluster. Only configurable for AWS clusters during creation. For GCP and Azure clusters, this value is ignored and the cloud provider default is used. Omit this attribute to use the server-side default based on machine type and storage size. The provisioned value may differ from the requested value.

machineType.description: Machine type identifier within the given cloud provider, e.g., m6.xlarge, n2-standard-4. This attribute requires a feature flag to be enabled. It is recommended to leave this empty and use `numVirtualCpus` to control the machine type.

numVirtualCpus.description: Number of virtual CPUs per node in the cluster.

privateNetworkVisibility.description: Set to true to assign private IP addresses to nodes. Required for CMEK and other advanced networking features. Clusters created with this flag will have advanced security features enabled.  This cannot be changed after cluster creation and incurs additional charges.  See [Create an Advanced Cluster](https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/cockroachcloud/create-an-advanced-cluster.html#step-6-configure-advanced-security-features) and [Pricing](https://www.cockroachlabs.com/pricing/) for more information.

storageGib.description: Storage amount per node in GiB.

supportsClusterVirtualization.description: supports_cluster_virtualization specifies whether an Advanced cluster is started with a virtual cluster architecture. This field is restricted to Private Preview usage; see our documentation for details: https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/cluster-virtualization-overview

internalDns.description: Internal DNS name of the cluster within the cloud provider's network. Used to connect to the cluster with PrivateLink or VPC peering.

name.description: Name of the region. Should match the region code used by the cluster's cloud provider.

nodeCount.description: Number of nodes in the region. Valid for Advanced clusters only.

primary.description: Set to true to mark this region as the primary for a serverless cluster. Exactly one region must be primary. Dedicated clusters expect to have no primary region.

privateEndpointDns.description: Domain name of the cluster for the private endpoint connection. This DNS name is used by GCP Private Service Connect to connect to the cluster.

s3VpcEndpointId.description: The ID of the AWS S3 VPC gateway endpoint for this region. Used to configure S3 bucket policies that restrict access to traffic from this VPC endpoint. Only populated for Advanced clusters on AWS.

sqlDns.description: DNS name of the cluster's SQL interface. Used to connect to the cluster with IP allowlisting.

uiDns.description: DNS name used when connecting to the DB Console for the cluster.

routingId.description: Cluster identifier in a connection string.

spendLimit.deprecationMessage: The `spendLimit` attribute is deprecated and will be removed in a future release of the provider. Configure 'usage_limits' instead.

upgradeType.description:
Dictates the behavior of CockroachDB major version upgrades. Manual upgrades are not supported on CockroachDB Basic. Manual or automatic upgrades are supported on CockroachDB Standard. If you omit the field, it defaults to `AUTOMATIC`. Allowed values are:
  * MANUAL
  * AUTOMATIC

provisionedVirtualCpus.description: Maximum number of vCPUs that the cluster can use.

requestUnitLimit.description: Maximum number of Request Units that the cluster can consume during the month.

storageMibLimit.description: Maximum amount of storage (in MiB) that the cluster can have at any time during the month.

internalDns.description: Internal DNS name of the cluster within the cloud provider's network. Used to connect to the cluster with PrivateLink or VPC peering.

name.description: Name of the region. Should match the region code used by the cluster's cloud provider.

nodeCount.description: Number of nodes in the region. Valid for Advanced clusters only.

primary.description: Set to true to mark this region as the primary for a serverless cluster. Exactly one region must be primary. Dedicated clusters expect to have no primary region.

privateEndpointDns.description: Domain name of the cluster for the private endpoint connection. This DNS name is used by GCP Private Service Connect to connect to the cluster.

s3VpcEndpointId.description: The ID of the AWS S3 VPC gateway endpoint for this region. Used to configure S3 bucket policies that restrict access to traffic from this VPC endpoint. Only populated for Advanced clusters on AWS.

sqlDns.description: DNS name of the cluster's SQL interface. Used to connect to the cluster with IP allowlisting.

uiDns.description: DNS name used when connecting to the DB Console for the cluster.

status.description: Describes the status of the current encryption key within the region.

authPrincipal.description: Principal to authenticate as in order to access the key.

createdAt.description: Indicates when the key was created.

status.description: Current status of this key.

type.description:
Type of encryption key. Current allowed values are:
  * AWS_KMS
  * GCP_CLOUD_KMS
  * NULL_KMS
  * AZURE_KEY_VAULT

updatedAt.description: Indicates when the key was last updated.

uri.description: Provider-specific URI pointing to the encryption key.

userMessage.description: Elaborates on the key's status and hints at how to fix issues that may have occurred during asynchronous key operations.

ccIdentity.description: Specifies how to map the fetched token identity to an identity in CockroachDB Cloud. In case of a regular expression for token_identity, this must contain a \1 placeholder for the matched content. Note that you will need to escape the backslash in the string as in the example usage (\\1).

tokenIdentity.description: Specifies how to fetch external identity from the token claim. A regular expression must start with a forward slash. The regular expression must be in RE2 compatible syntax. For further details, please see https://github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax.

channels.description: A list of CockroachDB log channels to include in this group.

logName.description: The name of the group, reflected in the log sink.

minLevel.description: The minimum log level to filter to this log group.

redact.description: Governs whether this log group should aggregate redacted logs if unset.

availabilityZoneIds.description: Availability Zone IDs of the private endpoint service. It is recommended, for cost optimization purposes, to create the private endpoint spanning these same availability zones. For more information, see data transfer cost information for your cloud provider.

aws.deprecationMessage: nested aws fields have been moved one level up. These fields will be removed in a future version

cloudProvider.description: Cloud provider associated with this service.

endpointServiceId.description: Server side ID of the private endpoint connection.

regionName.description: Cloud provider region code associated with this service.

status.description: Operation status of the service.

availabilityZoneIds.description: AZ IDs users should create their VPCs in to minimize their cost.

serviceId.description: Server side ID of the PrivateLink connection.

serviceName.description: AWS service name used to create endpoints.

availabilityZoneIds.description: Availability Zone IDs of the private endpoint service. It is recommended, for cost optimization purposes, to create the private endpoint spanning these same availability zones. For more information, see data transfer cost information for your cloud provider.

aws.deprecationMessage: nested aws fields have been moved one level up. These fields will be removed in a future version

cloudProvider.description: Cloud provider associated with this service.

endpointServiceId.description: Server side ID of the private endpoint connection.

regionName.description: Cloud provider region code associated with this service.

status.description: Operation status of the service.

availabilityZoneIds.description: AZ IDs users should create their VPCs in to minimize their cost.

serviceId.description: Server side ID of the PrivateLink connection.

serviceName.description: AWS service name used to create endpoints.

database.description: The database name in the fully qualified name of the objects to be restored. In the case of a job that restores `tpcc.public.warehouse`, this value would be `tpcc`.

schema.description: The schema name in the fully qualified name of the objects to be restored. In the case of a job that restores `tpcc.public.warehouse`, this value would be `public`.

tables.description: The table name in the fully qualified name of the objects to be restored. In the case of a job that restores `tpcc.public.warehouse`, this would value be `warehouse`.

intoDb.description: Optionally specifies a target database to restore the table into during a table restore job. If not set, the table is restored into the database it belonged to in the source backup.

newDbName.description: Optionally specifies the name of a new database to create as the target of a database restore job. If not set, the name defaults to the original database name from the source cluster.

schemaOnly.description: If set, only the schema without any user data is restored.

skipLocalitiesCheck.description: Allows the restore job to continue in the event that there are mismatched localities between the backup and target cluster. Useful when restoring multi-region tables to a cluster missing some localities.

skipMissingForeignKeys.description: Allows a table to be restored even if it has foreign key constraints referencing rows that no longer exist in the target cluster.

skipMissingSequences.description: Allows a table to be restored even if it contains a column whose `DEFAULT` value depends on a sequence. More information can be found [here](https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/show-sequences).

skipMissingViews.description: Allows the job to skip restoring views that cannot be restored because their dependencies are not included in the current restore job.

resourceId.description: ID of the resource. Required if the resourceType is 'FOLDER' or 'CLUSTER'. It should be omitted otherwise.

resourceType.description:
Type of resource. Allowed values are:
  * ORGANIZATION
  * CLUSTER
  * FOLDER

roleName.description:
Name of the role to grant. Allowed values are:
  * BILLING_COORDINATOR
  * ORG_ADMIN
  * ORG_MEMBER
  * CLUSTER_ADMIN
  * CLUSTER_OPERATOR_WRITER
  * CLUSTER_DEVELOPER
  * CLUSTER_CREATOR
  * FOLDER_ADMIN
  * FOLDER_MOVER
  * METRICS_VIEWER
  * CLUSTER_MONITOR
  * BILLING_VIEWER

resourceId.description: ID of the resource. Required if the resourceType is 'FOLDER' or 'CLUSTER'. It should be omitted otherwise.

resourceType.description:
Type of resource. Allowed values are: 
  * ORGANIZATION
  * CLUSTER
  * FOLDER

roleName.description:
Name of the role to grant. Allowed values are:
  * BILLING_COORDINATOR
  * ORG_ADMIN
  * ORG_MEMBER
  * CLUSTER_ADMIN
  * CLUSTER_OPERATOR_WRITER
  * CLUSTER_DEVELOPER
  * CLUSTER_CREATOR
  * FOLDER_ADMIN
  * FOLDER_MOVER
  * METRICS_VIEWER
  * CLUSTER_MONITOR
  * BILLING_VIEWER

asOfTime.description: The point in time (in UTC) the backup restores to.

id.description: The unique identifier associated with the backup.

clusterId.description: ID of the cluster the blackout window belongs to.

endTime.description: UTC end time in RFC3339 format (e.g. `2025-03-18T09:00:00Z`).

startTime.description: UTC start time in RFC3339 format (e.g. `2025-03-15T09:00:00Z`).

frequencyMinutes.description: The frequency of backups in minutes.

retentionDays.description: The number of days to retain backups for.

arn.description: The AWS IAM Role ARN that CockroachDB Cloud will assume in your account.

subscriptionId.description: The Azure subscription ID in the customer-owned tenant.

tenantId.description: The customer-owned Azure tenant ID that contains the subscription.

serviceAccountEmail.description: The customer-owned GCP service account email CockroachDB Cloud will impersonate.

cidrRange.description: The IPv4 range in CIDR format that is in use by the cluster. It is only set on GCP clusters and is otherwise empty.

diskIops.description: Number of disk I/O operations per second that are permitted on each node in the cluster. This value reflects the actual provisioned IOPS, which may differ from the value specified during cluster creation or update. A value of zero indicates the cloud provider-specific default.

machineType.description: Machine type identifier within the given cloud provider, ex. m6.xlarge, n2-standard-4.

numVirtualCpus.description: Number of virtual CPUs per node in the cluster.

privateNetworkVisibility.description: Indicates whether private IP addresses are assigned to nodes. Required for CMEK and other advanced networking features.

storageGib.description: Storage amount per node in GiB.

supportsClusterVirtualization.description: Specifies whether an Advanced cluster is started with a virtual cluster architecture.

internalDns.description: Internal DNS name of the cluster within the cloud provider's network. Used to connect to the cluster with PrivateLink or VPC peering.

name.description: Region code used by the cluster's cloud provider.

nodeCount.description: Number of nodes in the region. Will always be 0 for serverless clusters.

primary.description: Denotes whether this is the primary region in a serverless cluster. Dedicated clusters don't have a primary region.

privateEndpointDns.description: Domain name of the cluster for the private endpoint connection. This DNS name is used by GCP Private Service Connect to connect to the cluster.

s3VpcEndpointId.description: The ID of the AWS S3 VPC gateway endpoint for this region. Used to configure S3 bucket policies that restrict access to traffic from this VPC endpoint. Only populated for Advanced clusters on AWS.

sqlDns.description: DNS name of the cluster's SQL interface. Used to connect to the cluster with IP allowlisting.

uiDns.description: DNS name used when connecting to the DB Console for the cluster.

routingId.description: Cluster identifier in a connection string.

spendLimit.deprecationMessage: The `spendLimit` attribute is deprecated and will be removed in a future release of the provider. Configure 'usage_limits' instead.

upgradeType.description: Dictates the behavior of CockroachDB major version upgrades.

provisionedVirtualCpus.description: Maximum number of vCPUs that the cluster can use.

requestUnitLimit.description: Maximum number of Request Units that the cluster can consume during the month.

storageMibLimit.description: Maximum amount of storage (in MiB) that the cluster can have at any time during the month.

database.description: Database value to use in a connection URL.

host.description: Host value to use in a connection URL.

password.description: Password value to use in a connection URL.

port.description: Port value to use in a connection URL.

username.description: Username value to use in a connection URL.

backupEndTime.description: The timestamp at which the backup data was captured.

backupId.description: The ID of the managed backup used for this restore job.

clientErrorCode.description: Error code from the restore job, only populated if it has failed.

clientErrorMessage.description: Error message from the restore job, only populated if it has failed.

completedAt.description: The timestamp at which the restore job completed.

completionPercent.description: Decimal value showing the percentage of the restore job that has been completed. Value ranges from 0 to 1.

crdbJobId.description: The CockroachDB internal job ID for the restore job.

createdAt.description: The time at which the restore job was initiated.

destinationClusterName.description: The name of the cluster to which the restore is being applied.

id.description: The unique identifier associated with the restore job.

objects.description: The list of database objects (databases, tables) that were restored.

restoreOpts.description: Additional options controlling the behavior of the restore job.

sourceClusterName.description: The name of the cluster from which the backup was taken.

status.description: The current status of the restore job.

type.description: The type of object (to be) restored by this job.

database.description: The database name in the fully qualified name of the objects that were restored.

schema.description: The schema name in the fully qualified name of the objects that were restored.

tables.description: The list of table names in the fully qualified name of the objects that were restored.

intoDb.description: Specifies the target database to restore the table into during a table restore job. If not set, the table will be restored into the database it belonged to in the source backup.

newDbName.description: Specifies the name of the database to create during a database restore job. If not set, the name defaults to the original database name from the source cluster.

schemaOnly.description: If set, only the schema will be restored and no user data will be included.

skipLocalitiesCheck.description: Allows the restore job to continue in the event that there are mismatched localities between the backup and target cluster. Useful when restoring multi-region tables to a cluster missing some localities.

skipMissingForeignKeys.description: Allows a table to be restored even if it has foreign key constraints referencing rows that no longer exist in the target cluster.

skipMissingSequences.description: Allows a table to be restored even if it contains a column whose `DEFAULT` value depends on a sequence. (See https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/show-sequences)

skipMissingViews.description: Allows the job to skip restoring views that cannot be restored because their dependencies are not included in the current restore job.

provider.description:
The provider type for the cockroach package. By default, resources use package-wide configuration
settings, however an explicit `Provider` instance may be created and passed during resource
construction to achieve fine-grained programmatic control over provider settings. See the
[documentation](https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/programming-model/#providers) for more information.

apijwt.description:
The JWT from a JWT Issuer configured for the CockroachDB Cloud Organization.
In this case, the vanity name of the organization is required and can be provided using the `COCKROACH_VANITY_NAME` environment variable. If the JWT is mapped to multiple identities, the identity to impersonate should be provided using the `COCKROACH_USERNAME` environment variable, and should contain either a user email address or a service account ID.

apikey.description: The API key to access CockroachDB Cloud. If this field is provided, it is used and `apijwt` is ignored.

apijwt.description:
The JWT from a JWT Issuer configured for the CockroachDB Cloud Organization.
In this case, the vanity name of the organization is required and can be provided using the `COCKROACH_VANITY_NAME` environment variable. If the JWT is mapped to multiple identities, the identity to impersonate should be provided using the `COCKROACH_USERNAME` environment variable, and should contain either a user email address or a service account ID.

apikey.description: The API key to access CockroachDB Cloud. If this field is provided, it is used and `apijwt` is ignored.

cidrIp.description: IP address component of the [CIDR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing#CIDR_notation) range for this entry.

cidrMask.description: The [CIDR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing#CIDR_notation) notation prefix length. A number ranging from 0 to 32 indicating the size of the network. Use 32 to allow a single IP address.

name.description: Name of this allowlist entry. If left unset, it will inherit a server-side default.

sql.description: Set to 'true' to allow SQL connections from this CIDR range.

ui.description: Set to 'true' to allow access to the management console from this CIDR range.

cidrIp.description: IP address component of the [CIDR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing#CIDR_notation) range for this entry.

cidrMask.description: The [CIDR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing#CIDR_notation) notation prefix length. A number ranging from 0 to 32 indicating the size of the network. Use 32 to allow a single IP address.

name.description: Name of this allowlist entry. If left unset, it will inherit a server-side default.

sql.description: Set to 'true' to allow SQL connections from this CIDR range.

ui.description: Set to 'true' to allow access to the management console from this CIDR range.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering AllowList resources.

cidrIp.description: IP address component of the [CIDR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing#CIDR_notation) range for this entry.

cidrMask.description: The [CIDR](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing#CIDR_notation) notation prefix length. A number ranging from 0 to 32 indicating the size of the network. Use 32 to allow a single IP address.

name.description: Name of this allowlist entry. If left unset, it will inherit a server-side default.

sql.description: Set to 'true' to allow SQL connections from this CIDR range.

ui.description: Set to 'true' to allow access to the management console from this CIDR range.

createdAt.description: Creation time of the api key.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering ApiKey resources.

createdAt.description: Creation time of the api key.

clusterId.description: ID of the cluster the blackout window applies to.

endTime.description: The UTC end time for the blackout window in RFC3339 format (e.g. `2025-03-18T09:00:00Z`). Must fall within 14 days of `startTime` and no later than three months from now.

startTime.description: The UTC start time for the blackout window in RFC3339 format (e.g. `2025-03-15T09:00:00Z`). Must be scheduled at least seven days in advance.

clusterId.description: ID of the cluster the blackout window applies to.

endTime.description: The UTC end time for the blackout window in RFC3339 format (e.g. `2025-03-18T09:00:00Z`). Must fall within 14 days of `startTime` and no later than three months from now.

startTime.description: The UTC start time for the blackout window in RFC3339 format (e.g. `2025-03-15T09:00:00Z`). Must be scheduled at least seven days in advance.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering BlackoutWindow resources.

clusterId.description: ID of the cluster the blackout window applies to.

endTime.description: The UTC end time for the blackout window in RFC3339 format (e.g. `2025-03-18T09:00:00Z`). Must fall within 14 days of `startTime` and no later than three months from now.

startTime.description: The UTC start time for the blackout window in RFC3339 format (e.g. `2025-03-15T09:00:00Z`). Must be scheduled at least seven days in advance.

status.description: Status of client CA certs on a cluster.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering ClientCaCert resources.

status.description: Status of client CA certs on a cluster.

accountId.description: The cloud provider account ID that hosts the cluster. Needed for CMEK and other advanced features.

backupConfig.description:
The backup settings for a cluster.
 Each cluster has backup settings that determine if backups are enabled, how frequently they are taken, and how long they are retained for. Use this attribute to manage those settings.

cloudProvider.description:
Cloud provider used to host the cluster. Allowed values are:
  * GCP
  * AWS
  * AZURE

cockroachVersion.description: The major version of CockroachDB running on the cluster. This value can be used to orchestrate version upgrades. Supported for ADVANCED and STANDARD clusters (when `serverless.upgrade_type` set to 'MANUAL'). (e.g. v25.0)

creatorId.description: ID of the user who created the cluster.

customerCloudAccount.description: Cloud-specific details required to host the cluster in your own cloud account. Only one of `aws`, `gcp`, or `azure` may be specified. This feature is available in [Private Preview](https://www.cockroachlabs.com/docs/stable/cockroachdb-feature-availability). Contact your Cockroach Labs account team to enable this feature.

deleteProtection.description: Set to true to enable delete protection on the cluster. If unset, the server chooses the value on cluster creation, and preserves the value on cluster update.

fullVersion.description: The full version string of CockroachDB running on the cluster. (e.g. v25.0.1)

labels.description: Map of key-value pairs used to organize and categorize resources. If unset, labels will not be managed by Terraform. If set, labels defined in Terraform will overwrite any labels configured outside this platform.

operationStatus.description: Describes the current long-running operation, if any.

parentId.description: The ID of the cluster's parent folder. 'root' is used for a cluster at the root level.

plan.description: Denotes cluster plan type: 'BASIC' or 'STANDARD' or 'ADVANCED'.

state.description: Describes whether the cluster is being created, updated, deleted, etc.

upgradeStatus.description: Describes the status of any in-progress CockroachDB upgrade or rollback.

plan.description: Denotes cluster plan type: 'BASIC' or 'STANDARD' or 'ADVANCED'.

state.description: Describes whether the cluster is being created, updated, deleted, etc.

upgradeStatus.description: Describes the status of any in-progress CockroachDB upgrade or rollback.

additionalRegions.description: Once CMEK is enabled for a cluster, no new regions can be added to the cluster resource, since they need encryption key info stored in the CMEK resource. New regions can be added and maintained here instead.

status.description: Aggregated status of the cluster's encryption key(s).

clusterId.description: cluster_id identifies the cluster to which this egress private endpoint applies

endpointAddress.description: endpoint_address is the cloud-provider generated address (either IP address or DNS name) through which the endpoint is accessible

endpointConnectionId.description: endpoint_connection_id is the cloud service-specific ID for egress private endpoints. This connection ID is visible in CockroachDB and cloud service provider consoles as "VPC endpoint ID" and is used to uniquely identify the endpoint in external configurations

region.description: region represents the region that the endpoint will be created in

state.description:
state represents the current state of the endpoint. Potential values are:
  * PENDING
  * PENDING_ACCEPTANCE
  * AVAILABLE
  * DELETING
  * DELETED
  * REJECTED
  * FAILED
  * EXPIRED

targetServiceIdentifier.description: target_service_identifier represents the identifier of the target service that the CockroachDB endpoint connects to. For example, this would be the Service Name for AWS, Service Attachment for GCP, or ARN for AWS MSK

targetServiceType.description:
target_service_type represents the type of target service. Allowed values are:
  * PRIVATE_SERVICE
  * MSK_SASL_SCRAM
  * MSK_SASL_IAM
  * MSK_TLS

createdAt.description: The timestamp at which the replication stream was created.

failoverAt.description: The timestamp at which to perform failover. If not specified, failover will occur at the latest consistent replicated time. Cannot be used with failover_immediately.

failoverImmediately.description: If true, failover will occur immediately at the latest consistent replicated time. Cannot be used with failover_at.

primaryClusterId.description: ID of the primary cluster, which is the replication source.

replicatedTime.description: The timestamp indicating the point up to which data has been replicated.

retainedTime.description: The timestamp indicating the earliest time that the replication stream can failover to.

standbyClusterId.description: ID of the standby cluster, which is the replication target.

cockroach:index/privateEndpointConnectio.description:
Private endpoint connections allow customer applications to connect to a CockroachDB Cloud cluster without traversing the public internet. All application-database traffic remains within the cloud-provider network.

## Import

format: <cluster id>:<connection id>

```sh
$ pulumi import cockroach:index/privateEndpointConnection:PrivateEndpointConnection resource_name 1f69fdd2-600a-4cfc-a9ba-16995df0d77d:vpce-0c1308d7312217abc
```

cloudProvider.description: Cloud provider associated with this connection.

endpointId.description: Client side ID of the Private Endpoint Connection.

regionName.description: Cloud provider region code associated with this connection.

serviceId.description: Server side ID of the Private Endpoint Connection.

endpointId.description: Client side ID of the Private Endpoint Connection.

endpointId.description: Client side ID of the Private Endpoint Connection.

regionName.description: Cloud provider region code associated with this connection.

serviceId.description: Server side ID of the Private Endpoint Connection.

servicesMap.description: a map of services keyed by the region name

backupEndTime.description: The timestamp at which the backup data was captured.

backupId.description: ID of the managed backup from which data will be restored. If this value is not set, the restore job uses the most recent available backup on the source cluster.

clientErrorCode.description: Error code from the restore job, only populated if it has failed.

clientErrorMessage.description: Error message from the restore job, only populated if it has failed.

completedAt.description: The timestamp at which the restore job completed.

completionPercent.description: Decimal value showing the percentage of the restore job that has been completed. Value ranges from 0 to 1.

crdbJobId.description: The CockroachDB internal job ID for the restore job.

createdAt.description: The time at which the restore job was initiated.

destinationClusterId.description: ID of the cluster where the backup will be restored.

destinationClusterName.description: The name of the cluster to which the restore is being applied.

objects.description: The list of objects to restore. Required when the restore job type is `DATABASE` or `TABLE`.

restoreOpts.description: Additional options controlling the behavior of the restore job.

sourceClusterId.description: ID of the source cluster containing the managed backup to be restored.

sourceClusterName.description: The name of the cluster from which the backup was taken.

status.description: The current status of the restore job.

type.description: Type describes the scope of the restore job. When using a DATABASE or TABLE restore, additional details must be passed in the objects attribute. Allowed values are: `CLUSTER` or `DATABASE` or `TABLE`.

deferralPolicy.description:
The policy for delaying automated patch version upgrades after their release.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_30_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 30 days.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_60_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 60 days.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_90_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 90 days.
  - Set to `NOT_DEFERRED` to apply each upgrade soon after the patch is released.

deferredUntil.description: The earliest possible date and time for the next patch update, as calculated based on the configured `deferralPolicy`. The next patch is applied after this deferral date and time in a maintenance window..

deferralPolicy.description:
The policy for delaying automated patch version upgrades after their release.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_30_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 30 days.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_60_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 60 days.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_90_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 90 days.
  - Set to `NOT_DEFERRED` to apply each upgrade soon after the patch is released.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering VersionDeferral resources.

deferralPolicy.description:
The policy for delaying automated patch version upgrades after their release.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_30_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 30 days.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_60_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 60 days.
  - Set to `DEFERRAL_90_DAYS` to defer each upgrade by 90 days.
  - Set to `NOT_DEFERRED` to apply each upgrade soon after the patch is released.

deferredUntil.description: The earliest possible date and time for the next patch update, as calculated based on the configured `deferralPolicy`. The next patch is applied after this deferral date and time in a maintenance window..

inputs.description: A collection of arguments for invoking getBackups.

clusterId.description: The ID of the cluster containing the managed backups to be retrieved.

endTime.description: Ending timestamp of the range (exclusive) used to search for backups based on their restore point. If this field is provided, startTime must also be included in the request. Uses a date format with optional timestamp, for example: `2023-01-15` or `2023-01-15T10:30:00Z`.

limit.description: The maximum number of backups to return. If not set, only the first 500 backups will be returned.

sortOrder.description: Specifies the sort direction for the returned results, which are ordered based on the `asOfTime` field. Use `ASC` for ascending or `DESC` for descending order.

startTime.description: Beginning timestamp of the range (inclusive) used to search for backups based on their restore point. If this field is provided, endTime must also be included in the request. Uses a date format with optional timestamp, for example: `2023-01-15` or `2023-01-15T10:30:00Z`.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getBackups.

clusterId.description: The ID of the cluster containing the managed backups to be retrieved.

endTime.description: Ending timestamp of the range (exclusive) used to search for backups based on their restore point. If this field is provided, startTime must also be included in the request. Uses a date format with optional timestamp, for example: `2023-01-15` or `2023-01-15T10:30:00Z`.

id.description: The provider-assigned unique ID for this managed resource.

limit.description: The maximum number of backups to return. If not set, only the first 500 backups will be returned.

sortOrder.description: Specifies the sort direction for the returned results, which are ordered based on the `asOfTime` field. Use `ASC` for ascending or `DESC` for descending order.

startTime.description: Beginning timestamp of the range (inclusive) used to search for backups based on their restore point. If this field is provided, endTime must also be included in the request. Uses a date format with optional timestamp, for example: `2023-01-15` or `2023-01-15T10:30:00Z`.
